name: Kubernetes Extended
description: Extended Kubernetes protection beyond Kubernetes Baseline: broad kubectl delete/drain variants, destructive patches and scale-to-zero, helm rollback edge cases, and kustomize-driven deletions. Derived from the destructive_command_guard pattern library.

rules.name: Require access request for delete namespace
rules.description: kubectl delete namespace removes the entire namespace and ALL resources within it. Safer alternative: `kubectl delete ns {ns} --dry-run=client -o yaml` — Preview what would be deleted without making changes.

rules.name: Require access request for delete all namespaces
rules.description: kubectl delete with -A/--all-namespaces affects ALL namespaces. Very dangerous!

rules.name: Require access request for delete pvc
rules.description: kubectl delete pvc may permanently delete data if ReclaimPolicy is Delete. Safer alternative: `kubectl describe pvc {name}` — Check PVC status and usage before deleting.

rules.name: Require access request for delete pv
rules.description: kubectl delete pv may permanently delete the underlying storage.

rules.name: Require access request for delete force
rules.description: kubectl delete --force --grace-period=0 immediately removes resources without graceful shutdown. Safer alternative: `kubectl delete {resource} {name}` — Use default 30-second grace period for graceful shutdown.

rules.name: Require access request for kustomize delete
rules.description: kustomize build | kubectl delete removes all resources in the kustomization.

rules.name: Require access request for kubectl kustomize delete
rules.description: kubectl kustomize | kubectl delete removes all resources in the kustomization.

rules.name: Require access request for kubectl delete k
rules.description: kubectl delete -k removes all resources defined in the kustomization. Use --dry-run first.

rules.name: Require justification for delete all
rules.description: kubectl delete --all removes ALL resources of that type. Use --dry-run=client first. Safer alternative: `kubectl delete {resource} --all --dry-run=client` — Preview what would be deleted without making changes.

rules.name: Require justification for drain node
rules.description: kubectl drain evicts all pods from a node. Ensure proper pod disruption budgets.

rules.name: Require justification for taint noexecute
rules.description: kubectl taint with NoExecute evicts existing pods that don't tolerate the taint.

rules.name: Require justification for delete workload
rules.description: kubectl delete deployment/statefulset/daemonset removes the workload. Use --dry-run first.

rules.name: Require justification for scale to zero
rules.description: kubectl scale --replicas=0 stops all pods for the workload.

rules.name: Require justification for apply force
rules.description: kubectl apply --force deletes and recreates resources, causing downtime. Safer alternative: `kubectl apply -f {file}` — Apply without --force for in-place updates.

rules.name: Require justification for delete from directory
rules.description: kubectl delete -f with directories or --recursive deletes many resources at once. Safer alternative: `kubectl delete -f {specific-file}` — Delete from a specific file instead of directory.

rules.name: Require justification for rollback
rules.description: helm rollback reverts to a previous release. Use --dry-run to preview changes.

rules.name: Require justification for upgrade force
rules.description: helm upgrade --force deletes and recreates resources, causing downtime.

rules.name: Require justification for upgrade reset values
rules.description: helm upgrade --reset-values discards all previously set values.

rules.name: Warn on cordon node
rules.description: kubectl cordon marks a node unschedulable. Existing pods continue running.
